root_text.sn56.21:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vajjīsu viharati koṭigāme.
root_text.sn56.21:1.3: “catunnaṁ, bhikkhave, ariyasaccānaṁ ananubodhā appaṭivedhā evamidaṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ sandhāvitaṁ saṁsaritaṁ mamañceva tumhākañca.
root_text.sn56.21:2.2: Dukkhassa, bhikkhave, ariyasaccassa ananubodhā appaṭivedhā evamidaṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ sandhāvitaṁ saṁsaritaṁ mamañceva tumhākañca.
root_text.sn56.21:2.5: dukkhanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāya ariyasaccassa ananubodhā appaṭivedhā evamidaṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ sandhāvitaṁ saṁsaritaṁ mamañceva tumhākañca.
root_text.sn56.21:2.6: Tayidaṁ, bhikkhave, dukkhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ anubuddhaṁ paṭividdhaṁ, dukkhasamudayaṁ ariyasaccaṁ anubuddhaṁ paṭividdhaṁ, dukkhanirodhaṁ ariyasaccaṁ anubuddhaṁ paṭividdhaṁ, dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā ariyasaccaṁ anubuddhaṁ paṭividdhaṁ; ucchinnā bhavataṇhā, khīṇā bhavanetti; natthi dāni punabbhavo”ti.
root_text.sn56.21:3.2: Idaṁ vatvāna sugato athāparaṁ etadavoca satthā:

translation_text.sn56.21:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying in the land of the Vajjis at the village of Koṭi.
translation_text.sn56.21:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.sn56.21:1.3: “Mendicants, due to not understanding and not penetrating four noble truths, both you and I have wandered and transmigrated for such a very long time.
translation_text.sn56.21:2.5: and the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering.
translation_text.sn56.21:2.6: These noble truths of suffering, origin, cessation, and the path have been understood and comprehended. Craving for continued existence has been cut off; the leash to existence is ended; now there’ll be no more future lives.”
translation_text.sn56.21:3.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn56.21:3.2: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn56.21:4.3: we have transmigrated for a long time
translation_text.sn56.21:4.4: from one rebirth to the next.
translation_text.sn56.21:5.1: But now that these truths have been seen,
translation_text.sn56.21:5.2: the leash to existence is eradicated.
translation_text.sn56.21:5.3: The root of suffering is cut off,
translation_text.sn56.21:5.4: now there’ll be no more future lives.”
